body:
## Summary

Mirrors the CLI plugin's Codex integration for managed reviews. Codex runs as a 5th parallel source alongside the 4 Claude specialists **only when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set on the repo**; otherwise the review runs Claude-only with no cost/time overhead (behavior unchanged from v1.8.0).

## How it works

### Workflow side (\`managed-review.yml\`)
- New optional secret: \`OPENAI_API_KEY\` (\`required: false\`)
- Three Codex-gated steps run only when \`env.OPENAI_API_KEY != ''\`:
  1. Checkout target repo at \`refs/pull/<N>/head\` (reachable for open, merged, race-with-merge)
  2. \`npm install -g @openai/codex\`
  3. \`printenv OPENAI_API_KEY | codex login --with-api-key\` (headless auth)
- \`env.AIR_TARGET_REPO\` is the target-repo path when enabled, empty otherwise

### Driver side (\`review.py\`)
- \`run_codex_session(target_repo, base_sha)\` invokes \`codex review --base <sha>\` as an async subprocess with SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECS bound
- Runs as a 5th parallel task in Phase 1's \`asyncio.gather\` alongside the 4 Claude specialists
- \`specialist_labels\` list is dynamic — Codex appended when enabled, existing degradation logic handles all 5 uniformly
- Graceful skip paths (not failure): missing AIR_TARGET_REPO, missing \`codex\` binary, missing OPENAI_API_KEY, or user \`--no-codex\`
- New \`--no-codex\` flag for parity with the CLI plugin

## Cost impact

- Opt-out default: zero (no OPENAI_API_KEY = no-op)
- Opt-in: ~\$1–2 per review (Codex default model is gpt-5.4)

## Test plan

- [ ] Without OPENAI_API_KEY: review runs Claude-only, 4 specialists in parallel, no Codex steps appear in run log
- [ ] With OPENAI_API_KEY: target-repo checkout + codex install + codex login steps all succeed; 5 specialists in parallel; consolidated review reflects all 5
- [ ] \`--no-codex\` skips Codex even when OPENAI_API_KEY is set
- [ ] Codex failure (non-zero exit, API error) does NOT break the review — degraded note surfaces to the verifier
title: feat: add Codex as a 5th managed-review specialist (opt-in)
